ratios.source_note: Single-bidder award. LR derived from Cour des Comptes 2022 report § procurement irregularities; revised quarterly per AI-SAFETY-DOCTRINE-v1 §2.6.
Split tender below threshold — strong indicator of procurement-rigging per OECD-MAPS guidance.
No-bid emergency procurement. Many legitimate emergencies; LR conservative.
Late amendment changing scope/price.
Sole-source justification gap.
Uneven bid spread (collusion proxy).
Narrow technical specification favouring a single supplier.
Bid protest pattern across awards.
Debarment bypass — World Bank cross-debarment is a strong signal.
Shell-company indicators (sub-30-day incorporation, no website, generic registered address).
Nominee director linkage.
Jurisdiction shopping (BVI, Seychelles, Mauritius shell).
Sub-30-day pre-tender incorporation.
Co-incorporated cluster (cartel ring).
UBO mismatch — declared owner ≠ true beneficial owner.
PEP linkage to bidder, contracting officer, or beneficial owner.
Price > 30% above benchmark (FCFA-tier dependent).
Per-unit price anomaly across line items.
Quantity invoiced exceeds quantity specified.
Escalation diverging materially from official CPI.
Currency arbitrage — invoice rate above BEAC fixing.
Premature escalation-clause activation.
Ghost project — no satellite-detected activity during contract window. Highest LR in category D — physical absence of work is a near-deterministic indicator.
Project signed off as complete despite visibly incomplete works.
Site mismatch — work at coordinates >500 m from contracted location.
Quality deficit detected via satellite or independent inspection.
Progress fabrication — reported milestones decoupled from satellite-observed activity.
Direct sanctioned-entity exposure (OFAC SDN / EU / UN list hit). Very high LR.
Sanctioned-related entity (1-hop graph relation).
Payment routed through sanctioned jurisdiction.
Transaction with sanctioned PEP.
Round-trip payment loop.
Director ring across multiple shell companies.
Supplier circular flow.
Hub-and-spoke supplier topology.
Dense bidder network indicating coordination.
Backdated document detected via metadata or filesystem timestamps.
Signature image diverges from reference (similarity < 0.85).
Document metadata anomaly (unusual producer / template-mismatch).
Font anomaly indicating manual editing.
Award timestamped before tender close — strong temporal anomaly.
Amendment out of sequence in the documented chronology.
Holiday publication burst — weak alone, valuable as a corroborator.
